skill.name: forest-plot-styler
skill.description: Generates publication-ready forest plots for meta-analysis with customizable odds ratio points, confidence intervals, and subgroup analysis.
skill.summary: Automates forest plot creation from meta-analysis data with professional styling and subgroup support.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 91 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner pattern-matched against documentation content: markdown code blocks (backtick formatting) were flagged as shell execution, security checklist items mentioning '../' were flagged as path traversal, and text content was misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms. The actual Python code in scripts/main.py is a legitimate matplotlib-based forest plot generator. It reads local CSV/Excel files, generates plots, and writes output files. No network calls, no environment variable access, no data exfiltration, no dangerous code execution patterns were found. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged text content as cryptographic algorithm usage. All instances are in documentation text (markdown frontmatter, parameter tables, JSON audit results, and argparse help text). The skill does not implement any cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution Pattern
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged markdown code blocks (triple backticks) as shell command execution. These are documentation examples showing users how to run the script, not dynamic command execution. The Python script uses argparse for structured CLI argument parsing with no shell injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence Pattern
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged security checklist items that mention '../' as path traversal. Lines 253-254 say 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and lines 256-257 say 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. These are proactive security documentation items, not actual path traversal code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged standard development commands (python -m py_compile, argparse --help) as system reconnaissance. These are verification steps typical for data analysis skills and pose no security risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Pattern
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The flagged URL is a source attribution link to the skill author's GitHub repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is standard documentation practice and is not a network call or data exfiltration attempt.

content.user_title: Create Publication-Ready Forest Plots for Meta-Analysis
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually formatting forest plots for systematic reviews and meta-analyses. This skill automates the creation of customizable, high-resolution forest plots with odds ratios, confidence intervals, subgroup analysis, and publication-quality output.
actual_capabilities: Reads meta-analysis data from CSV and Excel files with automatic column validation
Generates customizable forest plots with odds ratio points, confidence intervals, and study labels
Supports subgroup analysis with inverse-variance weighted pooled effect calculations
Exports plots to PNG, PDF, or SVG at publication-quality resolution (up to 600 DPI)
Applies three preset visual styles: default, minimal, and dark theme
Calculates and displays pooled summary effects with diamond markers
limitations: Requires specific column names (study, or, ci_lower, ci_upper) in the input data file
Does not perform meta-regression, heterogeneity testing, or publication bias analysis
Cannot fetch or search for studies from external databases or APIs
Input data encoding must be UTF-8 and numeric columns must be valid numbers

use_cases.title: Medical Researcher Publishing a Systematic Review
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a Cochrane-style review needs publication-ready forest plots showing treatment effects across multiple clinical studies with proper formatting for journal submission.

use_cases.title: Data Analyst Creating Research Reports
use_cases.description: An analyst needs to quickly generate forest plots for internal reports and presentations without manually building charts in spreadsheet software.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Performing Thesis Meta-Analysis
use_cases.description: A student conducting a meta-analysis for their thesis generates forest plots with subgroup comparisons and custom formatting for their dissertation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Forest Plot from CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a forest plot using my data.csv file with default styling.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a properly formatted CSV file wanting a quick default plot

prompt_templates.title: Custom Styled Forest Plot
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a forest plot from meta_analysis.csv with custom odds ratio point color #E63946, confidence interval color #457B9D, point size 10, and title 'Treatment Effect Analysis'. Output as high-resolution PNG.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with specific publication formatting requirements for colors and sizing

prompt_templates.title: Subgroup Analysis with Pooled Effects
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a forest plot from clinical_trials.csv with subgroup analysis on the 'treatment_group' column. Use summary color #F4A261 and diamond shape for pooled effects. Export as SVG vector graphic.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher comparing treatment effects across different patient subgroups

prompt_templates.title: Dark Theme Presentation Plot
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dark-themed forest plot from my_data.xlsx file. Set odds ratio points to size 10 in blue, confidence intervals in light gray with linewidth 3. Title: 'Meta-Analysis of Treatment Effects'. Output as 16x10 inch PDF at 600 DPI.
prompt_templates.scenario: User preparing presentation slides with dark background needing matching plot theme

output_examples.input: Generate a forest plot from clinical_trials.csv with default styling.
output_examples.output: Forest plot generated successfully. The plot shows 12 studies with odds ratios ranging from 0.55 to 1.50. Study B (OR 0.72, 95% CI 0.55 to 0.94) was the only statistically significant result. The pooled effect estimate (OR 0.92, 95% CI 0.78 to 1.08) suggests a small but non-significant overall treatment effect. Output saved as forest_plot.png.

output_examples.input: Generate a forest plot from meta_data.csv with subgroup analysis on the drug_type column and dark theme.
output_examples.output: Forest plot generated with subgroup analysis for 2 drug groups. Drug A subgroup (4 studies, pooled OR 0.78, 95% CI 0.62 to 0.98) showed significant effect. Drug B subgroup (3 studies, pooled OR 1.05, 95% CI 0.82 to 1.34) showed no significant effect. Dark theme applied. Output saved as subgroup_forest.png.

best_practices: Validate your input data has at least 3 studies for meaningful forest plot visualization.
Specify output format PNG for quick review and SVG or PDF for journal submission.
Use the weight column when available to show study contribution through proportional point sizes.
anti_patterns: Do not fabricate study data to create a forest plot - use only real research data.
Do not use forest plots to compare studies with incompatible outcome measures or populations.
Do not ignore confidence intervals crossing the reference line - explicitly mention non-significant results.

faq.question: What data format does the forest plot styler require?
faq.answer: CSV or Excel files with specific columns: study, or, ci_lower, ci_upper. Weight and subgroup columns are optional.

faq.question: Can I customize colors and sizes of plot elements?
faq.answer: Yes. You can set point color, CI line color, line width, font size, and summary effect color and shape through command parameters.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: PNG, PDF, and SVG. Default is PNG at 300 DPI. Use the format parameter to change the output type.

faq.question: Does this skill perform statistical analysis beyond visualization?
faq.answer: It calculates inverse-variance weighted pooled effect sizes with confidence intervals for subgroups and overall summary.

faq.question: Can I use this with Excel files instead of CSV?
faq.answer: Yes. Both .xlsx and .xls files are supported for reading meta-analysis data.

faq.question: What makes a good forest plot dataset?
faq.answer: At minimum you need study names, odds ratios, and confidence interval bounds. Weight values help show study contribution through point sizes.
